channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.
item.content:encoded:
The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
item.description: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.
item.content:encoded:
She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

(Image credit: Tovia Smith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Gas prices keep rising, but do big oil companies plan to drill more? Not so far
item.description: The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Campaign staffers tell NPR they make 'thousands' betting on their own candidates
item.description: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.content:encoded: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: How the petrodollar regime came to be, and what losing it would mean for the U.S.
item.description: The global oil market has supported the U.S. dollar for over 50 years. But the system shows signs of cracking under the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: The global oil market has supported the U.S. dollar for over 50 years. But the system shows signs of cracking under the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: CNN Anchor Christiane Amanpour remembers Ted Turner
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with CNN's Christiane Amanpour about broadcaster Ted Turner's legacy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with CNN's Christiane Amanpour about broadcaster Ted Turner's legacy.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: How Silicon Valley's new tech right has profited by aligning with MAGA
item.description: Atlantic writer George Packer discusses how tech venture capitalists, who are heavily invested in AI and cryptocurrency, aligned with Trump and influenced policies related to their own investments.
item.content:encoded: Atlantic writer George Packer discusses how tech venture capitalists, who are heavily invested in AI and cryptocurrency, aligned with Trump and influenced policies related to their own investments.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: 'A trailblazer, a rabble-rouser, a do-gooder': CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
item.description: Turner had a larger-than-life personality, and used it to launch the country's first 24/7 all-news network.
item.content:encoded:
Turner had a larger-than-life personality, and used it to launch the country's first 24/7 all-news network.

(Image credit: Rick Diamond/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Have you lost or are worried about losing SNAP benefits? NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.content:encoded:
NPR wants to hear from people impacted by changes to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A trip to Europe? In this economy? Expensive flights keep vacations closer to home
item.description: Travel advisers are seeing Americans picking cheaper domestic travel destinations over Europe.
item.content:encoded:
Travel advisers are seeing Americans picking cheaper domestic travel destinations over Europe.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
